name: Integration Builder — <<INTEGRATION_NAME>>
outcome_goal: A manifest-declared integration for <<INTEGRATION_NAME>> is registered in integration_registry, loads via praxis workflow discover, and exposes its MVP capabilities.
anti_requirements: Do NOT write a custom Python handler when a declarative TOML manifest suffices — manifest-only is the default.
Do NOT hardcode secrets. Auth must resolve via resolve_secret (.env → Keychain service=praxis → env var) or a credential_ref.
Do NOT exceed the MVP scope agreed in the scoping phase — extra endpoints go in a follow-up spec.

jobs.label: discover_existing
jobs.description: Scan for prior <<INTEGRATION_SLUG>> code, manifests, or tool-catalog rows; decide build-new vs extend.
jobs.outcome_goal: A note at artifacts/integration_builder_<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>/00_discovery.md that lists: any existing manifest/adapter, the closest analogue to copy, and the verdict (new-manifest | extend-manifest | custom-adapter).
jobs.prompt:
Orient with `praxis workflow query status` and `praxis workflow discover "<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>"`. Then:

1. Confirm there is no existing integration for <<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>. Search:
   - `Code&DBs/Integrations/manifests/*.toml`
   - `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/integrations/` for custom Python adapters
   - `integration_registry` rows via `praxis workflow tools call praxis_integration --input-json '{"action":"list"}' --yes`
2. Read the manifest schema to confirm supported auth modes and capability shape:
   - `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/integration_manifest.py` — IntegrationManifest, AuthShape, ActionSpec
   - `Code&DBs/Integrations/manifests/webhook-example.toml` — the canonical example
3. Pick the closest analogue manifest to copy from. Write `artifacts/integration_builder_<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>/00_discovery.md` with: prior art found (or none), analogue manifest chosen, verdict, and any gaps that would block a pure-manifest build (e.g. URL path interpolation, custom auth flow).

jobs.label: research_api
jobs.description: Resolve <<INTEGRATION_NAME>> auth mode, base URL, minimum scopes, and candidate endpoints.
jobs.outcome_goal: A note at artifacts/integration_builder_<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>/01_api_contract.md with auth model (bearer/api_key/oauth2), base URL, scopes, and ≥ 8 candidate endpoints (method + full URL + body shape + description).
jobs.prompt:
Using the vendor's public docs at <<AUTH_DOCS_URL>> and adjacent reference pages, produce `artifacts/integration_builder_<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>/01_api_contract.md` containing:

- **Auth**: which mode (`api_key` / `oauth2` / `env_var`), what header / token format, what env var name (use UPPER_SNAKE convention: e.g. `<<SECRET_ENV_VAR>>`), any scope list.
- **Base URL**: fully qualified origin, e.g. `https://api.example.com/v1`.
- **Endpoint inventory**: at least 8 candidate endpoints. Each row must include: action slug (`snake_case_verb_object`), HTTP method, full URL (https://…), one-line description, and the expected body shape (or `n/a`).
- **Gaps**: any capability that the declarative manifest cannot express (URL path params, multipart upload, webhook callback, pagination helpers). Flag these for the scoping phase.

jobs.label: scope_mvp
jobs.description: Trim the endpoint inventory to the minimum useful MVP; decide per-endpoint body shape.
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/integration_builder_<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>/02_mvp_scope.md names the MVP actions (no more than 12), each with action slug, method, URL, and whether it needs a body_template.
jobs.prompt:
Read 01_api_contract.md. Produce `artifacts/integration_builder_<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>/02_mvp_scope.md` selecting an MVP set with the smallest possible coverage that still lets a caller do useful end-to-end work. Heuristics:

- Prefer read + create + search over update/delete in v1.
- Keep total capabilities ≤ 12.
- Skip any endpoint that needs URL path params (/{id}) until the manifest loader supports them — note that in an "out of scope" section.
- For each selected action, decide: does it need a `body_template` (static shape with `{{placeholder}}`s) or can we pass the caller's args straight through as the JSON body?
- List an explicit "out of scope — follow-up" section so we can file the next builder run cleanly.

jobs.label: write_manifest
jobs.description: Emit Code&DBs/Integrations/manifests/<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>.toml from the MVP scope and verify it parses.
jobs.outcome_goal: Code&DBs/Integrations/manifests/<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>.toml exists, parses via load_manifests(), and yields an IntegrationManifest with id=<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>> and the agreed capabilities.
jobs.prompt:
Read 02_mvp_scope.md and write `Code&DBs/Integrations/manifests/<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>.toml`.

Manifest rules (from runtime/integration_manifest.py):
- Required top blocks: `[integration]` (id, name, description, provider, icon), `[auth]`, and one or more `[[capabilities]]` arrays.
- `integration.id` must match `^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]{0,126}[a-zA-Z0-9]$`.
- `auth.kind` ∈ {`env_var`, `api_key`, `oauth2`}. For bearer PATs use `api_key` + `env_var = "<<SECRET_ENV_VAR>>"`.
- Each `[[capabilities]]` row needs `action` (snake_case), `method` (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/HEAD/OPTIONS), `path` (full `https://…` URL), `description`. Optional: `body_template` (dict with `{{placeholder}}`s) and `response_extract` (single top-level key).
- Max 50 capabilities, max 64 KB file size.
- Add a header comment with the keychain command: `security add-generic-password -U -a praxis -s <<SECRET_ENV_VAR>> -w <token>`.

After writing, verify the manifest parses:
  PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -c "from runtime.integration_manifest import load_manifests; ms=[m for m in load_manifests() if m.id=='<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>']; assert ms, 'manifest missing'; print(len(ms[0].capabilities), 'caps')"
jobs.verify_command: PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -c "from runtime.integration_manifest import load_manifests; ms=[m for m in load_manifests() if m.id=='<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>']; assert ms and ms[0].auth_shape.env_var=='<<SECRET_ENV_VAR>>' and len(ms[0].capabilities)>=1"

jobs.label: patch_infra_gaps
jobs.description: Fix or file bugs for any manifest-loader / credential-resolution gaps surfaced during discovery.
jobs.outcome_goal: Every gap flagged in 00_discovery.md or 02_mvp_scope.md either (a) has a landed fix in runtime/integration_manifest.py or adapters/, or (b) is filed as a praxis bug with a clear repro.
jobs.prompt:
Review `artifacts/integration_builder_<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>/00_discovery.md` and `02_mvp_scope.md` for any items labelled "gap" or "out of scope — loader limitation". For each:

1. If the fix is < 30 LOC and localized to `runtime/integration_manifest.py` or `adapters/keychain.py`/`adapters/credentials.py`, land the fix and run `PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -m pytest --noconftest -q Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_integration_manifest.py` (create the test file if none exists).
2. Otherwise, file a bug: `praxis workflow tools call praxis_bugs --input-json '{"action":"create","title":"...","evidence_refs":["artifacts/integration_builder_<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>/..."]}' --yes`.

If no gaps were flagged, this job exits with a no-op log and the verify_command still succeeds.

jobs.label: register_and_verify
jobs.description: Sync the registry, confirm the integration appears, and smoke-test one capability if a token is present.
jobs.outcome_goal: integration_registry has a row for <<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>; praxis workflow discover "<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>" returns the manifest; a dry-run call to one capability succeeds or cleanly reports credential.env_var_missing.
jobs.prompt:
Run the sync and verify:

1. `praxis workflow tools call praxis_reload --input-json '{}' --yes` (or `praxis workflow discover reindex --yes` if reload is unavailable).
2. Query the registry:
   - `praxis workflow tools call praxis_integration --input-json '{"action":"describe","slug":"<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>"}' --yes`
3. Discover: `praxis workflow discover "<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>"` must return the integration.
4. Dry-run a capability:
   - Pick the safest `list_*` capability from the manifest.
   - `praxis workflow tools call praxis_integration --input-json '{"action":"execute","slug":"<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>","capability":"<action>","args":{}}' --yes`
   - Success = HTTP 200, OR reason_code `credential.env_var_missing` (expected when the keychain secret isn't populated on the build host).

Write a short summary to `artifacts/integration_builder_<<INTEGRATION_SLUG>>/04_verify.md` recording: registry row present (y/n), discover hit (y/n), smoke-test status, and the exact keychain command the operator needs to run to store the secret.
